notes: All 27 skills GEPA-optimized. None improved over seed (all scored 0.5 on val, non-deterministic CLI routing is the bottleneck). Hand-crafted descriptions are near-optimal.

tdd-workflow.best_description: Use this skill when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. Enforces test-driven development with 80%+ coverage including unit, integration, and E2E tests.

agentic-engineering.best_description: Use when operating as an agentic engineer on complex tasks. Provides eval-first execution, task decomposition into smaller testable pieces, and cost-aware model routing.

ai-first-engineering.best_description: Use when setting up AI-first development workflows for teams. Defines the operating model where AI agents generate implementation output, with review and quality processes.

api-design.best_description: Use when designing REST API endpoints, choosing HTTP methods and status codes, implementing pagination, filtering, error responses, versioning, or rate limiting.

backend-patterns.best_description: Use when building backend APIs, designing database access layers, implementing caching or authentication, or optimizing server-side Node.js, Express, or Next.js API routes.

coding-standards.best_description: Use when writing or reviewing TypeScript, JavaScript, React, or Node.js code. Enforces naming conventions, immutability patterns, error handling, async patterns, and function size limits.

content-hash-cache-pattern.best_description: Use when caching file processing results to avoid reprocessing unchanged files. Implements SHA-256 content hash caching — path-independent, auto-invalidating, with service layer separation.

continuous-agent-loop.best_description: Use when running autonomous agent loops, setting up continuous agent pipelines, or implementing quality gates with evals and recovery controls for long-running tasks.

continuous-learning.best_description: Use when extracting reusable patterns from sessions, learning from completed work, or managing instincts. Creates atomic instincts with confidence scoring that evolve into skills, commands, and agents.

cost-aware-llm-pipeline.best_description: Use when optimizing LLM API costs, routing tasks to cheaper models, setting up budget tracking, implementing retry logic, or enabling prompt caching for AI pipelines.

database-migrations.best_description: Use when creating database migrations, altering schemas, performing data migrations, implementing zero-downtime deployments, or managing rollbacks across PostgreSQL, MySQL, and ORMs (Prisma, Drizzle, Django, TypeORM).

deployment-patterns.best_description: Use when setting up CI/CD pipelines, configuring health checks, implementing rolling or blue-green deployments, creating production readiness checklists, or deploying to Kubernetes.

docker-patterns.best_description: Use when writing Dockerfiles, configuring Docker Compose, setting up container networking, managing volumes, or implementing multi-stage builds for secure containers.

e2e-testing.best_description: Use when writing Playwright end-to-end tests, setting up Page Object Model, configuring E2E test pipelines, managing test artifacts (screenshots, videos, traces), or fixing flaky tests.

eval-harness.best_description: Use when creating evaluation frameworks for agent workflows, defining eval test cases, measuring agent quality and performance, or implementing eval-driven development (EDD).

frontend-patterns.best_description: Use when building React components, managing frontend state, optimizing UI performance, implementing lazy loading, error boundaries, or accessible interfaces in React/Next.js apps.

iterative-retrieval.best_description: Use when searching for code across a large codebase and initial search is too broad. Progressively refines context retrieval queries to find relevant files, solving the subagent context problem.

plankton-code-quality.best_description: Use when setting up auto-formatting and linting on every file edit. Enforces write-time code quality with Plankton -- formatting, linting, and agent-powered fixes via hooks.

postgres-patterns.best_description: Use when writing PostgreSQL queries, designing database schemas, creating indexes, implementing Row Level Security, or optimizing query performance with Supabase patterns.

python-patterns.best_description: Use when writing Python code. Enforces PEP 8 style, type hints, Pythonic idioms (EAFP, comprehensions, dataclasses), proper exception handling, and async patterns.

python-testing.best_description: Use when writing Python tests with pytest, setting up test fixtures, mocking dependencies, parametrizing test cases, or measuring code coverage. Enforces TDD methodology for Python.

regex-vs-llm-structured-text.best_description: Use when deciding between regex and LLM for parsing or extracting data from structured text. Decision framework: start with regex, add LLM only for low-confidence edge cases.

search-first.best_description: Use when starting a new implementation task. Research existing tools, libraries, and patterns before writing custom code. Search for solutions first, then implement.

security-review.best_description: Use this skill when adding authentication, handling user input, working with secrets, creating API endpoints, or implementing payment/sensitive features. Provides comprehensive security checklist and patterns.

strategic-compact.best_description: Use when context window is getting large, conversation needs compacting, or you want to preserve important context through task phases. Suggests strategic compaction at logical intervals.

ui-ux-pro-max.best_description: Use when generating design systems, choosing UI styles, selecting color palettes and font pairings, or getting stack-specific UI guidelines. Contains 67 styles, 96 palettes, 57 fonts across 13 tech stacks with BM25 search.

verification-loop.best_description: Use when verifying code changes before committing or pushing. Runs build, tests, lint, and type checks in a comprehensive verification loop to catch errors early.

bugs_fixed: timeout-scoring: timeouts now fall through to scoring logic instead of returning 0.0 blindly
timeout-increased: 120s -> 180s to account for gemini CLI startup latency
